CAPTION: a newspaper article with a bus and a map
OCR: Delhi, TN, Maha lead
e-bus sales surge in H1

Govt incentives, charging infrastructure also fuel e-bus demand in Odish

Mai ilk
manas.pimpalkhare@hindustantimes.com
NEW DELHI

elhi, Tamil Nadu, Maharash
tra and Odisha saw a signifi-
cant rise in e-bus sales in the
first half of FY26 (April-Sep-
tember) compared with sales
in the same period last fiscal, propelling
the country's total e-bus sales to rise 37%
to 2,241, from 1,635 in the previous fiscal.

This gains significance as the Centre
focuses on electrifying public transport to
reduce vehicular emissions, at a time
when demand for e-buses is shaky. The
Vahan portal data showed thatin the first
half of this fiscal, Delhi’s e-bus sales rose
69% to 938, the highest in India. During
the same period in the previous fiscal, 555
e-buses were sold.

Maharashtra's e-bus sales increased
48% to 665 units, from 448 units in the
previous fiscal year. In Tamil Nadu, 251
e-buses were sold within this period,
much higher than the 5 units sold in the
corresponding period of FY25.

Odisha's e-bus sales quadrupled to 125
in HI FY26 from 31 in the previous fiscal.
E-busesare currently used for commutes
within cities and in select areas for travel
between cities, but only a few states have
shown significant adoption of this
clean method of public trans-
portation. The average price
ofan e-busis about %1 crore.
The subsidy under the PM
E-Drive scheme is in the
range of 25-35 lakh per bus,
provided the manufacturer
meets the localisation criteria.

Akey factor determining the
sales of e-buses is the availability of
charging infrastructure in urban areas.
India had approximately 29,200 public
charging stations in August, according to
agovernment disclosure in the Lok Sabha.

Charger-to-bus ratios are integral in the
electrification of public transport, along
with the integration of chargers at bus

E-mobility boost

India’s e-bus sales rose 37% to 2,241 units in H1 FY26, led by Delhi (938), with
strong growth also in Maharashtra (665), Tamil Nadu (251), and Odisha (125).

Note: Data from Telangana is not

depots, domain experts said. "Global
benchmarks show that countries with
robust bus electrification programmes,
like China, maintain a charger-to-bus
ratio of-1:3, whereas in India it is closer to
1:8-1:12, indicating the need for acceler-
ated infrastructure rollout," Rand-
heer Singh, former director of
electric mobility at NITI
Aayog, and currently chief
executive officer, ForeSee
Advisors, said.
Additionally, under PM
E-Drive scheme, the heavy
industries ministry plans to
cut upfront costs for establishing
over 72,000 public charging sta-
tions nationwide. To promote intercity
travel, the government plans to reduce the
upfront cost of procuring e-buses for
operation on select highway corridors,
including Delhi-Jaipur, Delhi-Agra, Del-
hi-Chandigarh, and Mumbai-Pune.
According to Surya Khurana, managing

across Ahmedabad (1600), Surat (1600),

The firm has quietly emerged as a serious force
in India’s e-bus market.

DNA through its parent, Pinnacle Industries

OCR: PMI Electro bags bulk of e-bus
orders under PM E-DRIVE

NITIN KUMAR
New Delhi, December 24

PMI ELECTRO HAS emerged
as the largest beneficiary of
the country’s biggest electric
bus tender, securing orders
for 5,210 buses, nearly half of
the 10,900 vehicles offered
under the Prime Minister
Electric Drive Revolution in
Innovative Vehicle Enhance-
ment (PM E-DRIVE) scheme,
according to official sources.
The company currently
accounts for about a quarter
of the 4,239 electric buses
sold in the country in 2025
and has anexisting orderbook
of around 3,000 buses. With
the latest allocation, PMI Elec-
tro has strengthened its lead.
EKA Mobility, a subsidiary
of Pinnacle Industries,
emergedas thesecond-largest
winner in the tender, securing
3,485 buses. Olectra followed
with 1,785 buses, while the
remaining 420 buses were
awarded toaconsortiumled by
Anthony Travels, sources said.
Several established bus
manufacturers failed to
secure any orders. Tata
Motors, VE Commercial Vehi-
cles and JBM Auto did not win
allocations in the tender.
Ashok Leyland, through its
subsidiary OHM Global
Mobility, has contested its
exclusion and has moved the
Delhi High Court, arguing

CV COMPANY.

PITHAMPUR PLANT - EXPORT ENGINE

VECV's Pithampur plant exports medium duty engines to Volvo
fleets across Europe and Asia. It is a global manufacturing
partner — not just an Indian CV maker.

UJJAIN + $544 CR VOLVO AMT PLANT

Volvo just invested $544 crores in a new AMT plant at Ujjain.
This plant will supply automatic transmissions to Volvo trucks
across Asia and Oceania.
